examples.title: Recursive Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Standard recursive inorder traversal visiting Left-Root-Right
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Inorder(node)
  IF node != NULL THEN
    Inorder(node.left)      // Visit left subtree
    PRINT node.data         // Visit root
    Inorder(node.right)     // Visit right subtree
  END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Recursively traverses left subtree, then processes current node, then right subtree. For BST, produces sorted output. Uses call stack implicitly.

examples.title: Level Order Traversal
examples.description: Iterative level order traversal using queue
examples.explanation: Uses queue (FIFO) to process nodes level by level. Dequeue current node, enqueue its children. Ensures parents processed before children, left before right.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w is max width, O(n) worst case

examples.title: Binary Search Tree Search
examples.description: Recursive search in BST following left<root<right property
examples.explanation: Compares key with root, if smaller goes left, if larger goes right. Average case O(log n) for balanced BST, worst case O(n) for skewed tree.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) for recursive, O(1) for iterative

examples.title: Constructing Tree from Traversals
examples.description: Java implementation showing tree construction from preorder and inorder
examples.explanation: First element of preorder is root. Find this root in inorder. Elements left of root in inorder form left subtree, right form right subtree. Recursively build. Time O(n²) without optimization, O(n) with map.
